data.body:
PASS-1 review — T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay], for the FOLLOW-ON plan "Reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry (coverage.metrics) on the workflow plan-review gate": CLEAN — no findings.

T1 is an overlay that engages only when a plan crosses a bright-line (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, a NOVEL architectural pattern, a performance/scalability target, or a migration). This plan crosses none, and where it makes a design choice it explicitly grounds it in established in-repo PRIOR ART rather than inventing or repackaging — so under the overlay's ANTI-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search") it does not fire.

(a) Relevant prior art considered — not reinventing/repackaging:
- The metrics shape itself is a REINSTATEMENT, not a new invention: it reproduces db7b AC5's exact dict {det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path} that bespoke run_review produced before B-RETIRE removed it. The plan names this prior art explicitly ("resolving the AC's two candidates"; claim_path "RETAINED, matching the db7b dict exactly").
- The data SOURCE is an existing rebar capability, not a new mechanism: the workflow interpreter already records per-step duration_ms (src/rebar/llm/workflow/interpreter.py:388,489,502; persisted by executor.py:655,672-673), and the batch finder already emits criteria_count. The plan reconstructs the split from these existing recorder timings in one gate-dispatch helper (gate_dispatch.py:121-171) rather than re-threading bespoke per-pass timers — i.e. it reuses an existing substrate.

(b) Novel-pattern justification (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three): no novel architecture is introduced. Reconstructing telemetry post-hoc from the recorder's own step timings is the natural reuse of an existing field, not a new pattern needing justification. There is no novel substrate to weigh against an established approach.

(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: every "rebar provides X" claim is verifiable in-tree, not asserted — the interpreter's per-step duration_ms (interpreter.py:388,489), step-kind tiering (precheck DET vs agent/batch LLM, gate_dispatch.py:152-159), and the batch finder's criteria_count (gate_dispatch.py:156-157) all exist and are exercised. No unresolved capability assertion.

Affirmations (T1 checklist all satisfied):
- Relevant prior art considered — YES: the deliverable is an explicit reinstatement of db7b AC5's retired telemetry, reusing the interpreter's existing recorder step-timings as the source.
- Novel pattern justified — N/A: no novel architecture; post-hoc reconstruction from existing per-step timings is reuse, not a new pattern.
- Unverified capability assertions resolved — YES: every named capability (duration_ms, step-kind tiering, criteria_count) is present and exercised in-tree.

No T1 bright-line fires; emitting no findings is the correct outcome.
